sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 70

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 70:1
items.html:
(to the question) MANTZEPACH
The discernments of Masach and Aviut of a Partzuf that remained in it from the time of its Katnut are called MANTZEPACH. And by this name we distinguish between the MAN of the lower one, which are cleaved to the AHP of the Partzuf of the Nukva, and the MAN of the Nukva herself, which remain in her from the time of her own Ibur [conception]. For the MAN of the lower one must be incorporated in the Masach of the Ibur of the Nukva herself, and then they receive the level of the Ibur; hence the MAN of the Ibur are incorporated in the MANTZEPACH of the Nukva, and the Nukva raises them as MAN to ZA, and then the embryo, too, is built in its own MAN, and receives its level. This is the secret of "it eats what its mother eats," and this is the secret of "the embryo is its mother's thigh," for the MAN of the embryo are incorporated in her MANTZEPACH, and have no recognition of their own. See Answer 69. (Item 73)
